headline: RM18 billion highway concession terms remain classified for 30 years
context: Concession agreements for major toll highways worth RM18 billion remain classified under OSA, with full terms not scheduled for disclosure until 2035 at the earliest.

cards.text: RM18 billion in highway concession terms classified secret for 30 years
cards.sub: Malaysians pay tolls under contracts they are legally forbidden from reading

cards.h: OSA Section 2A classifies concession agreements as government confidential documents
cards.s: Disclosing toll road terms carries criminal penalties including imprisonment
cards.d: OSA Section 2A classifies concession agreements as government confidential documents Disclosing toll road terms carries criminal penalties including imprisonment

cards.h: Toll collection exceeded RM7.2 billion in 2025 paid entirely by Malaysian motorists
cards.s: Consumers bear full cost but have zero visibility into profit-sharing arrangements
cards.d: Toll collection exceeded RM7.2 billion in 2025 paid entirely by Malaysian motorists Consumers bear full cost but have zero visibility into profit-sharing arrangements

cards.h: Indonesia publishes all highway concession terms on public procurement portal
cards.s: Regional neighbour proves infrastructure concession transparency is achievable
cards.d: Indonesia publishes all highway concession terms on public procurement portal Regional neighbour proves infrastructure concession transparency is achievable

cards.text: When the people who pay can't see the contract, the contract isn't serving the people
Like hiring a contractor who bills you monthly but locks the invoice in a safe for 30 years
cards.sub: The public pays through tolls but cannot evaluate whether the terms are fair until 2035

cards.text: Should all highway concession agreements be declassified and published immediately?
